title: Internet of things (IoT) enabled wireless sensor system enabling process control, predictive maintenance of electrical distribution networks, liquid and gas pipelines and monitoring of air pollutants including nuclear, chemical, and biological agents using attached and/or embedded passive electromagnetic sensors
abstract: The invention relates generally to an Internet of Things (IoT) enabled wireless sensor system using attached and/or embedded passive electromagnetic sensors (PES) with distribution hardware. One embodiment of this invention includes a wireless sensor system, which permits process control and predictive maintenance on a utility's electrical transmission and distribution grid. Another embodiment includes a wireless sensor system, which permits process control and predictive maintenance of liquid or gas through a pipeline. Another embodiment includes a wireless sensor system, which permits measurement of breathable air pollutants. Furthermore, a method of manufacturing a protective passive electromagnetic sensor pod and passive electromagnetic sensor equipped distribution hardware components is provided.
claims: 7. A method of utilizing modified acoustic waves to enable process control and predictive maintenance comprising steps of: a. harvesting of a first electromagnetic wave by a passive electromagnetic sensor; b. converting said electromagnetic wave to an acoustic wave; c. modifying said acoustic wave based on a phenomena to be measured to create a first modified acoustic wave; d. transmitting said first modified acoustic wave to a microprocessor of said passive electromagnetic sensor or an electromagnetic controller communicator; e. whereby said microprocessor or electromagnetic controller communicator computes a first value for said phenomena to be measured from said first modified acoustic wave; f. transmitting said first value to a network, wherein the network includes: a plurality of passive electromagnetic sensors; a plurality of electromagnetic controller communicators; at least one user computing resource or at least one user computer; and at least one supercomputer with artificial intelligence means, whereby at least one of the electromagnetic controller communicators of whereby said network processes said first value and determines whether said value is anomalous by comparison of said first value with a known relational module wherein the artificial intelligence means is configured to learn phenomena patterns representing normal and anomalous conditions at least based on phenomena measurement values measured by the plurality of passive electromagnetic sensors and to generate the relational module for determining a first anomalous condition wherein said network issues execution commands to any segment of a utility grid; and g. whereby programming logic of said network enables process control and predictive maintenance commands based on said comparison of said first value with said known relational module to maintain wellness of said utility grid.
